title: Robes for George’s dream team
description: GEORGE NEWS - The newly elected DA mayor of...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - The newly elected DA mayor of George, Browen Johnson, promises a transparent governance delivering real solutions and building a just, safe and prosperous city.


> With these words, Johnson set the tone for a new chapter in George’s leadership - a chapter that began only after a disrupted and extended council sitting last Friday, 21 November.


The voting and inauguration ultimately took place about three and a half hours after the meeting’s scheduled start, following repeated disruptions by opposition councillors and several 30-minute adjournments called by the speaker to restore order.

During the same Special Council Meeting, Gert van Niekerk of the FF Plus was elected as deputy mayor and the former mayor, Jackie von Brandis (DA), as chief whip of council.

The election of Section 79 committee members was confirmed on Monday 24 November, during the continuation of proceedings.

Johnson said he takes up the responsibility “with humility and a firm commitment to all our residents”, adding that strengthening service delivery and accountable governance will be central to his term. Van Niekerk pledged his support for the new administration, highlighting stability, fiscal discipline and equitable services across all wards.

The new leadership team: Jackie von Brandis (chief whip of council), Mayor Browen Johnson and Deputy Mayor Gert van Niekerk after their election at the Special Council Meeting.

The executive team: Speaker Sean Snyman, Deputy Mayor Gert van Niekerk, Mayor Browen Johnson and Municipal Manager Godfrey Louw.


> Johnson’s new Mayoral Committee:


Executive leadership

• Executive mayor: Ald Browen Johnson

• Executive deputy mayor: Ald Gert van Niekerk

• Speaker: Cllr Sean Snyman

• Chief whip of council: Ald Jackie von Brandis.

Members of the Mayoral Committee (MMCs)

• Social Development (Libraries, Sport and Customer Care): Cllr Melvin Roelfse

• Community Safety and Mobility: Ald Daniel Cronje

• Planning and Development: Cllr Gert Niehaus

• Human Settlements: Cllr Jean Safers

• Electro-technical Services: Cllr Monique Simmers

• Corporate Services: Cllr Johan du Toit

• Human Resources: Cllr Jayze Jantjies

• Civil Engineering Services: Cllr Donovan Gultig

• Community Services (Refuse, Environment and Parks): Cllr Erika Brown

• Financial and Strategic Services: Ald Iona Kritzinger.

Section 79 Committees

• Municipal Public Accounts Committee (MPAC): Chris Neethling

• George Integrated Public Transport Network (GIPTN): Given Masase.

Mayor Browen Johnson is robed with the mayoral regalia by Municipal Manager Godfrey Louw and Speaker Sean Snyman.

Taking the reins: The newly elected mayor, Browen Johnson, shakes hands with the municipal manager, Godfrey Louw, after the official announcement.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
